message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: Tests/test_image_quantize.py,src/PIL/Image.py
note: Implementing sort_palette parameter for Image.quantize() - adds custom palette sorting after quantization

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: My feature: add error_threshold parameter to quantize(). I need to edit Image.py (quantize method) and its test file. What is your feature and which files do you need? If you also need Image.py, let's discuss a way to split edits within it (e.g. I only touch quantize()/related helper, you touch elsewhere) or agree on order.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: Tests/test_image_quantize.py,src/PIL/Image.py
your_files: Tests/test_image_quantize.py,src/PIL/Image.py
note: Understood, we both touch quantize() and its test file - true disjointness isn't possible since we're each adding a new keyword param. My feature: sort_palette=None kwarg on Image.quantize() that reorders the palette post-quantization (predefined modes like 'luminance'/'rgb'). I'll add it as a purely additive new kwarg appended after existing params, applied at the end of quantize() right before return, and add new standalone test functions (not editing your test functions). This should keep edits non-overlapping in practice even though we share files. Proceeding to implement now - shout if you need a different approach.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Our features both touch quantize() so full file disjointness isn't possible - we each need Image.py + the test file. Proposal to avoid clobbering: I'll add error_threshold as a new keyword-only-style param appended AFTER all existing params in the quantize() signature/docstring, with new logic added as a self-contained block that doesn't touch your sort_palette logic. I'll add my tests as new, separately-named test functions appended at the end of test_image_quantize.py. Please do the same for sort_palette (append param after mine, keep your logic block separate, append your tests at end of file). This keeps our diffs in non-overlapping regions as much as possible. Reply ACCEPT if that works for you.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: Tests/test_image_quantize.py,src/PIL/Image.py
your_files: Tests/test_image_quantize.py,src/PIL/Image.py
note: Agreed. I'll append sort_palette param after your error_threshold in quantize() signature/docstring, implement it as a self-contained block right before the final return, and append my new test functions at the end of test_image_quantize.py. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT

your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Agreed - proceeding to implement error_threshold now: new param appended after existing quantize() params, self-contained logic block, new standalone test functions only.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Implementation complete: added error_threshold param appended after dither in quantize() signature/docstring, self-contained _quantize_with_error_threshold() helper method, and 4 new standalone test functions appended at end of test_image_quantize.py. All 12 tests pass. Ready for you to layer sort_palette on top.

type: DONE
my_files: Tests/test_image_quantize.py,src/PIL/Image.py
note: Implemented sort_palette=None kwarg on quantize() (accepts 'luminance' or 'rgb'), added as new self-contained block right before the final return, using the existing remap_palette() method to reorder without changing appearance. Added new standalone test functions at the end of test_image_quantize.py. Did not touch the quantize() signature elsewhere except appending sort_palette as the last param - should compose cleanly with your error_threshold addition.
